message:
fix(cass): add size limit for fallback session parsing

Problem:
- handleSessionExportFailure reads entire session file into memory
- Called when cass export fails (binary missing, parse error, etc.)
- Very large session files (>10MB) could cause OOM or severe slowdowns
- No upper bound existed on file size before readFile call

Solution:
- Add fs.stat check before reading file in fallback mode
- Skip files larger than 10MB with warning log
- Return null to indicate parsing was skipped
- Callers already handle null (session is simply not processed)

Rationale for 10MB limit:
- Typical agent sessions are 100KB-2MB
- Files >10MB are likely corrupted, binary, or not real sessions
- Matches the "reasonable session" heuristic used elsewhere
- Prevents accidental resource exhaustion on malformed data

The warning message includes actual file size in MB for debugging.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
